english: ception (prop.: a veiling of the head) Mil.; steṅ-γyógṣ (steng g.yogs), stod-γyógṣ (stod g.yogs) upper-garment, mantle, toga, smad-γyógṣ (smad g.yogs) trowsers, breeches Tar. — 2. cover, envelope, γyógṣ-ċan (g.yogs can) having a cover.
Pth.; Cs. = γyó-ċan (g.yo can) crafty; perh. also fornicator, as γyón-ma (g.yon ma), acc. to Lex. and Sch.: harlot.

left, γyón-ma (g.yon ma) the left hand, γyón-na (g.yon na) on the left, to the left, γyón-du (g.yon du) towards the left, γyón-nas (g.yon nas) from the left; γyón-lógs (g.yon logs) the left side or hand, γyon-lág-byed-pa (g.yon lag byed pa) Pth. left-handed, γyon-rú (g.yon ru) Sch. the left wing, of an army.
pf. γyobs (g.yobs) to move about, to swing, brandish, γśòg-pa (g.shog pa) the wings; rkaṅ-lág γyób-pa (rkang lag g.yob pa) to kick, to strike, with the arms and legs.

1. sail, γyor-yól (g.yor yol) id Cs., γyor-śiṅ (g.yor shing) sail-yard Cs., also mast, in a rather obscure description of a ship in Zam., where the sail is called dar (dar), cloth. — 2. wave, billow, rgyá-mťsoi (rgya mtsho'i) Glr. Note. Tibetan writers knowing of ships and navigation about as much as a blind man of colours, the obscurity of passages relating to such matters may easily be accounted for.
1. v. γyúr-ba (g.yur ba). — 2. v. γyár-ba (g.yar ba). — 3. v. yor (yor).

1. prov. for γyas (g.yas), in γyos-skór (g.yos skor) circumambulation from left to right (so that the right side is towards the person or object that is reverentially to be saluted) Wdṅ. — 2. v. γyo-ba (g.yo ba).
1. the consonant r, always pronounced with the tongue. — 2. num. fig.: 25.
stands for: 1. rá-ba (ra ba), 2. rá-ma (ra ma), 3. rá-mda (ra mda'), 4. rá-ro (ra ro).

(cf. ru (ru)) 1. horn W. *rá-cò* (ra co) id. — 2. sting e.g. of the scorpion. — 3. Sch.: 'the inward side, the horn-side, of a bow'. — rwa-ċan (rwa can) horned. — rwa-snyíṅ (rwa snying) the pith of a horn Cs. — rwa-myúg (rwa myug) 'the first germ of seed that appears after sowing' Cs.; rwá-rtsa (rwa rtsa) 'the root or bottom of a horn' Cs., rwá-rtse (rwa rtse) 'the top or point of a horn' Cs., rwá-tsa (rwa tsa) S.g. (?).

in comp. rag (rag), brass, rá-gan-gyi búm-pa (ra gan gyi bum pa), rag-búm (rag bum) brass cup, can, vessel, rag-dúṅ (rag dung) a brass trumpet; rag-skyá (rag skya) Sch.: white-copper, packfong, German silver.
an officinal root Med., Sch.: carrot.
Cs.: 'a small weight, a drachm (60 grains)'; but रती (not to be found in Will.) is prob. the Hindi word for रत्तिका, the seed or grain of Abrus precatorius, as a weight about = 2 grains.

help, assistance (Cs. also: companion, assistant), rá-mda bód-pa (ra mda' bod pa) to cry out for help Glr., rá-mdar sbrón-pa (ra mdar sbron pa) Cs. to call (upon a person) for assistance, ra ˳dégs-pa (ra 'degs pa) W. *ram tág-ċe* (ram tag ce) (cf. żabs ˳dégs-pa (zhabs 'degs pa)) to help, to assist Sch., ra ˳drén-pa (ra 'dren pa) id. Mil. nt.; rá-mda-pa (ra mda' pa) helper, assistant Glr.; rá-mdai dpuṅ-tšóg (ra mda'i dpung tshog) auxiliary forces or army Cs.
